ABCD_SubMod_StatAnalysis

Felicia Hardi

2025-07-25

Load packages

library(broom)
library(cluster)
library(dplyr)
library(emmeans)
library(factoextra)
library(ggeffects)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(grid)
library(Hmisc)
library(interactions)
library(kableExtra)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(knitr)
library(magrittr)
library(NbClust)
library(psych)
library(purrr)
library(table1)
library(tidyverse)
library(viridis)

Create dfs

agnes_res_env$clust = as.factor(agnes_res_env$clust)
agnes_res_env$mri_info_manufacturer = as.factor(agnes_res_env$mri_info_manufacturer)
agnes_res_env$site_id_l = as.factor(agnes_res_env$site_id_l)
agnes_res_env$sex = agnes_res_env$demo_sex_v2
agnes_res_env$sex = ifelse(agnes_res_env$sex==3, NA, agnes_res_env$sex)
agnes_res_env$sex = as.factor(agnes_res_env$sex)

# bpm long (6 timepoints)
# create long version of bpm
agnes_res_env_bpm_long = 
  agnes_res_env %>% 
  select(src_subject_id, clust, 
         bpm_y_scr_internal_r_6_month_follow_up_arm_1, bpm_y_scr_internal_r_1_year_follow_up_y_arm_1, 
         bpm_y_scr_internal_r_18_month_follow_up_arm_1, bpm_y_scr_internal_r_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1,
         bpm_y_scr_internal_r_30_month_follow_up_arm_1, bpm_y_scr_internal_r_3_year_follow_up_y_arm_1, 
         rsfmri_meanmotion, mri_info_manufacturer, site_id_l, rel_family_id, sex, weights, 
         pub_base, demo_sex_v2) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("bpm_y_scr"), names_pattern = "bpm_y_scr_internal_r_(.*)_arm_1",
               names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "bpm_int") %>% 
  cbind((agnes_res_env %>% select(src_subject_id, 
                                  bpm_y_scr_external_r_6_month_follow_up_arm_1, 
                                  bpm_y_scr_external_r_1_year_follow_up_y_arm_1, 
                                  bpm_y_scr_external_r_18_month_follow_up_arm_1, 
                                  bpm_y_scr_external_r_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1,
                                  bpm_y_scr_external_r_30_month_follow_up_arm_1, 
                                  bpm_y_scr_external_r_3_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
           pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("bpm_y_scr"), names_pattern = "bpm_y_scr_external_r_(.*)_arm_1",
                        names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "bpm_ext") %>% select(bpm_ext)), 
        (agnes_res_env %>% select(src_subject_id, 
                                  bpm_y_scr_totalprob_r_6_month_follow_up_arm_1, 
                                  bpm_y_scr_totalprob_r_1_year_follow_up_y_arm_1, 
                                  bpm_y_scr_totalprob_r_18_month_follow_up_arm_1, 
                                  bpm_y_scr_totalprob_r_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1,
                                  bpm_y_scr_totalprob_r_30_month_follow_up_arm_1,
                                  bpm_y_scr_totalprob_r_3_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
           pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("bpm_y_scr"), names_pattern = "bpm_y_scr_total_r_(.*)_arm_1",
                        names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "bpm_tot")) %>% select(bpm_tot)) %>% 
  mutate(timepoint = as.factor(timepoint))
agnes_res_env_bpm_long = left_join(agnes_res_env_bpm_long,
                                   age %>% pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("age_"), names_pattern = "age_(.*)_arm_1",
                                                        names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "age") %>% 
                                     select(src_subject_id, timepoint, age))
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, timepoint)`
agnes_res_env_bpm_long = left_join(agnes_res_env_bpm_long, agnes_res_env %>% 
                                     select(src_subject_id, age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1))
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id)`
# create a new df with changeage only
changeage = age %>% pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("changeage_"), names_pattern = "changeage_(.*)",
                                 names_to = "change_timepoint", values_to = "changeage") %>%
  select(src_subject_id, change_timepoint, changeage) %>% filter(str_starts(change_timepoint, "from6mo_to"))
changeage = changeage %>% mutate(timepoint = if_else(change_timepoint == "from6mo_to1yr", "1_year_follow_up_y",
                                                     if_else(change_timepoint == "from1yr_to18mo", "18_month_follow_up",
                                                             if_else(change_timepoint == "from18mo_to2yr", "2_year_follow_up_y",
                                                                     if_else(change_timepoint == "from2yr_to30mo", "30_month_follow_up",
                                                                             if_else(change_timepoint == "from30mo_to3yr", "3_year_follow_up_y",
                                                                                     NA_character_))))))
agnes_res_env_bpm_long = left_join(agnes_res_env_bpm_long, changeage)
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, timepoint)`
agnes_res_env_bpm_long = agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% mutate(changeage = if_else(!is.na(age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1) & is.na(change_timepoint), 0, changeage))

# create long version of the brain
agnes_res_env_brain_long = 
  agnes_res_env %>% 
  select(src_subject_id, clust, 
         dmn_dmn_baseline_year_1_arm_1, dmn_dmn_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1, 
         rsfmri_meanmotion, mri_info_manufacturer, site_id_l, rel_family_id, sex, weights, 
         pub_base, demo_sex_v2) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = ends_with("arm_1"), names_pattern = "dmn_dmn_(.*)_arm_1",
               names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "dmn") %>%
  cbind(agnes_res_env %>% 
          select(src_subject_id, sal_sal_baseline_year_1_arm_1, sal_sal_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
          pivot_longer(cols = ends_with("arm_1"), names_pattern = "sal_sal_(.*)_arm_1",
                       names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "sal") %>% select(sal)) %>% 
  cbind(agnes_res_env %>% 
          select(src_subject_id, fpn_fpn_baseline_year_1_arm_1, fpn_fpn_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
          pivot_longer(cols = ends_with("arm_1"), names_pattern = "fpn_fpn_(.*)_arm_1",
                       names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "fpn") %>% select(fpn)) %>% 
  cbind(agnes_res_env %>% 
          select(src_subject_id, dmn_sal_baseline_year_1_arm_1, dmn_sal_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
          pivot_longer(cols = ends_with("arm_1"), names_pattern = "dmn_sal_(.*)_arm_1",
                       names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "dmn_sal") %>% select(dmn_sal)) %>% 
  cbind(agnes_res_env %>% 
          select(src_subject_id, dmn_fpn_baseline_year_1_arm_1, dmn_fpn_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
          pivot_longer(cols = ends_with("arm_1"), names_pattern = "dmn_fpn_(.*)_arm_1",
                       names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "dmn_fpn") %>% select(dmn_fpn)) %>% 
  cbind(agnes_res_env %>% 
          select(src_subject_id, sal_fpn_baseline_year_1_arm_1, sal_fpn_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
          pivot_longer(cols = ends_with("arm_1"), names_pattern = "sal_fpn_(.*)_arm_1",
                       names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "sal_fpn") %>% select(sal_fpn)) %>% 
  mutate(timepoint = as.factor(timepoint))
agnes_res_env_brain_long = left_join(agnes_res_env_brain_long, agnes_res_env %>% 
                                       select(src_subject_id, age_baseline_year_1_arm_1))
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id)`
agnes_res_env_brain_long = left_join(agnes_res_env_brain_long, age %>%
                                       pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("age_"), names_pattern = "age_(.*)_arm_1",
                                                    names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "age") %>%
                                       filter(timepoint=="baseline_year_1" | timepoint=="2_year_follow_up_y") %>%
                                       select(src_subject_id, timepoint, age))
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, timepoint)`
agnes_res_env_brain_long = left_join(agnes_res_env_brain_long,
                                     age %>% pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("changeage_"), names_pattern = "changeage_(.*)",
                                                          names_to = "change_timepoint", values_to = "changeage") %>% 
                                       select(src_subject_id, change_timepoint, changeage) %>%
                                       filter(change_timepoint == "frombase_to2yr"), by="src_subject_id") 

# combined df
agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long = 
  left_join(agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% 
              mutate(timepoint = case_when(
                timepoint == "6_month_follow_up" ~ "baseline_year_1", TRUE ~ timepoint)) %>% 
              select(-c(change_timepoint, changeage)), 
            agnes_res_env_brain_long %>% select(-c(age)))
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, clust, rsfmri_meanmotion,
## mri_info_manufacturer, site_id_l, rel_family_id, sex, weights, pub_base,
## demo_sex_v2, timepoint)`

Change in bpm across time

# baseline levels
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + (1 | site_id_l), data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% filter(timepoint == "6_month_follow_up"), weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                              Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## clust                       124.014  62.007     2 7004.0  51.043 < 2.2e-16 ***
## age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1  34.924  34.924     1 7677.9  28.749 8.478e-08 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
base1 = (lmer(bpm_int ~ clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + (1 | site_id_l), data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% filter(timepoint == "6_month_follow_up"), weights = weights))
emmeans(base1, pairwise ~ clust, adjust="fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 7832' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 7832)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 7832' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 7832)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emmeans
##  clust emmean     SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  1       1.59 0.0434 Inf      1.50      1.67
##  2       2.09 0.0644 Inf      1.97      2.22
##  3       2.04 0.0599 Inf      1.93      2.16
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2  -0.5095 0.0626 Inf  -8.145  <.0001
##  clust1 - clust3  -0.4588 0.0589 Inf  -7.794  <.0001
##  clust2 - clust3   0.0507 0.0747 Inf   0.679  0.4972
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
anova(lmer(bpm_ext ~ clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + (1 | site_id_l), data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% filter(timepoint == "6_month_follow_up"), weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                             Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## clust                       141.48  70.738     2 7078.0 64.2603 < 2.2e-16 ***
## age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1   7.54   7.540     1 7712.2  6.8497  0.008883 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
base2 = (lmer(bpm_ext ~ clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + (1 | site_id_l), data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% filter(timepoint == "6_month_follow_up"), weights = weights))
emmeans(base2, pairwise ~ clust, adjust="fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 7861' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 7861)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 7861' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 7861)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emmeans
##  clust emmean     SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  1       1.70 0.0397 Inf      1.63      1.78
##  2       2.35 0.0602 Inf      2.23      2.47
##  3       2.02 0.0556 Inf      1.91      2.13
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2   -0.649 0.0595 Inf -10.908  <.0001
##  clust1 - clust3   -0.316 0.0557 Inf  -5.668  <.0001
##  clust2 - clust3    0.333 0.0708 Inf   4.708  <.0001
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
# test if the change in bpm differs by groups
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                              Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## age                         153.291 153.291     1 36601 188.883 < 2.2e-16 ***
## I(age^2)                    178.645 178.645     1 36639 220.123 < 2.2e-16 ***
## clust                        16.960   8.480     2 44063  10.449 2.904e-05 ***
## age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1  26.667  26.667     1 17450  32.858 1.008e-08 ***
## changeage                    13.166  13.166     1 35450  16.223 5.643e-05 ***
## I(age^2):clust               15.306   7.653     2 41839   9.430 8.045e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
mod1 = (lmer(bpm_int ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
test(emtrends(mod1, pairwise ~ clust, var = "age", adjust="fdr"))
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 44580' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 44580)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 44580' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 44580)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emtrends
##  clust age.trend     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1         0.147 0.0116 Inf  12.678  <.0001
##  2         0.219 0.0212 Inf  10.325  <.0001
##  3         0.098 0.0192 Inf   5.094  <.0001
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2  -0.0718 0.0232 Inf  -3.092  0.0030
##  clust1 - clust3   0.0491 0.0215 Inf   2.281  0.0225
##  clust2 - clust3   0.1209 0.0279 Inf   4.332  <.0001
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
anova(lmer(bpm_ext ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                             Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## age                         34.877  34.877     1 35829 59.9225 1.013e-14 ***
## I(age^2)                    43.388  43.388     1 35860 74.5442 < 2.2e-16 ***
## clust                        9.337   4.669     2 43455  8.0211 0.0003289 ***
## age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1  6.868   6.868     1 17407 11.7991 0.0005940 ***
## changeage                   34.789  34.789     1 34891 59.7705 1.094e-14 ***
## I(age^2):clust               3.992   1.996     2 40532  3.4291 0.0324257 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
mod2 = (lmer(bpm_ext ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
emtrends(mod2, pairwise ~ clust, var = "age", adjust="fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 44322' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 44322)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 44322' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 44322)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emtrends
##  clust age.trend      SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  1        0.0815 0.00986 Inf    0.0622     0.101
##  2        0.1249 0.01810 Inf    0.0894     0.160
##  3        0.1164 0.01640 Inf    0.0842     0.149
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2 -0.04336 0.0199 Inf  -2.184  0.0868
##  clust1 - clust3 -0.03493 0.0184 Inf  -1.896  0.0868
##  clust2 - clust3  0.00843 0.0239 Inf   0.353  0.7240
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
# is the quad better fit than the linear model? had to remove weights bc of convergence
lin1 = (lmer(bpm_int ~ age * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude))
lin2 = (lmer(bpm_ext ~ age * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude))
poly1 = (lmer(bpm_int ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude))
poly2 = (lmer(bpm_ext ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude))

anova(lin1, poly1)
## refitting model(s) with ML (instead of REML)
## Data: agnes_res_env_bpm_long
## Models:
## lin1: bpm_int ~ age * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l)
## poly1: bpm_int ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l)
##       npar    AIC    BIC logLik -2*log(L)  Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## lin1    11 178506 178601 -89242    178484                         
## poly1   12 178245 178349 -89110    178221 263.23  1  < 2.2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(lin2, poly2)
## refitting model(s) with ML (instead of REML)
## Data: agnes_res_env_bpm_long
## Models:
## lin2: bpm_ext ~ age * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l)
## poly2: bpm_ext ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l)
##       npar    AIC    BIC logLik -2*log(L)  Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## lin2    11 163449 163545 -81714    163427                         
## poly2   12 163358 163462 -81667    163334 93.734  1  < 2.2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Risk and protective factors predicting mental health

mod1 = (lmer(bpm_int ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
agnes_res_env_bpm_long$resid_intmod = resid(mod1)
mod2 = (lmer(bpm_ext ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
agnes_res_env_bpm_long$resid_extmod = resid(mod2)
agnes_res_env_bpm_long = left_join(agnes_res_env_bpm_long, agnes_res_env[1:32])
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, clust)`
agnes_res_env_bpm_long[21:50] = scale(agnes_res_env_bpm_long[21:50])

agnes_res_env_bpm_long_c1 = agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% filter(clust==1)
agnes_res_env_bpm_long_c2 = agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% filter(clust==2)
agnes_res_env_bpm_long_c3 = agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% filter(clust==3)

env = colnames(agnes_res_env[3:32])

# mental health (internalizing)
mh_int_env = lapply(env, function(env) {
  lmer(reformulate(termlabels = c(env, "(1 | src_subject_id)"), response = "resid_intmod"),
       data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long_c1, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights) |>
    summary()})
names(mh_int_env) = env
mh_int_env_summ = lapply(env, function(env) {
  model_summary = mh_int_env[[env]]
  coeffs = model_summary$coefficients
  row_name = env
  int_row = coeffs[rownames(coeffs) == row_name, , drop = FALSE]})
names(mh_int_env_summ) = names(mh_int_env)
mh_int_env_summ1 = lapply(mh_int_env_summ, function(coeffs) {
  coeffs_df = as.data.frame(coeffs)
  coeffs_df$term = rownames(coeffs_df) 
  coeffs_df}) %>% bind_rows(.id = "env") 
mh_int_env_summ1$clust = 1

mh_int_env2 = lapply(env, function(env) {
  lmer(reformulate(termlabels = c(env, "(1 | src_subject_id)"), response = "resid_intmod"),
       data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long_c2, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights) |>
    summary()})
names(mh_int_env2) = env
mh_int_env_summ2 = lapply(env, function(env) {
  model_summary = mh_int_env2[[env]]
  coeffs = model_summary$coefficients
  row_name = env
  int_row = coeffs[rownames(coeffs) == row_name, , drop = FALSE]})
names(mh_int_env_summ2) = names(mh_int_env2)
mh_int_env_summ2 = lapply(mh_int_env_summ2, function(coeffs) {
  coeffs_df = as.data.frame(coeffs)
  coeffs_df$term = rownames(coeffs_df) 
  coeffs_df}) %>% bind_rows(.id = "env") 
mh_int_env_summ2$clust = 2

mh_int_env3 = lapply(env, function(env) {
  lmer(reformulate(termlabels = c(env, "(1 | src_subject_id)"), response = "resid_intmod"),
       data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long_c3, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights) |>
    summary()})
names(mh_int_env3) = env
mh_int_env_summ3 = lapply(env, function(env) {
  model_summary = mh_int_env3[[env]]
  coeffs = model_summary$coefficients
  row_name = env
  int_row = coeffs[rownames(coeffs) == row_name, , drop = FALSE]})
names(mh_int_env_summ3) = env
mh_int_env_summ3 = lapply(mh_int_env_summ3, function(coeffs) {
  coeffs_df = as.data.frame(coeffs)
  coeffs_df$term = rownames(coeffs_df) 
  coeffs_df}) %>% bind_rows(.id = "env") 
mh_int_env_summ3$clust = 3

mh_env_summ_int = rbind(mh_int_env_summ1, mh_int_env_summ2, mh_int_env_summ3)
mh_env_summ_int$padj = p.adjust(mh_env_summ_int$`Pr(>|t|)`, method="fdr")
mh_env_summ_int$padj = round(mh_env_summ_int$padj, digits=3)
mh_env_summ_int = data.frame(mh_env_summ_int, row.names = NULL)
mh_env_summ_int

## 85                                 Lead.risk     3 0.932

# mental health (externalizing)
mh_env = lapply(env, function(env) {
  lmer(reformulate(termlabels = c(env, "(1 | src_subject_id)"), response = "resid_extmod"),
       data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long_c1, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights) |>
    summary()})
names(mh_env) = env
mh_env_summ = lapply(env, function(env) {
  model_summary = mh_env[[env]]
  coeffs = model_summary$coefficients
  row_name = env
  int_row = coeffs[rownames(coeffs) == row_name, , drop = FALSE]})
names(mh_env_summ) = names(mh_env)
mh_env_summ1 = lapply(mh_env_summ, function(coeffs) {
  coeffs_df = as.data.frame(coeffs)
  coeffs_df$term = rownames(coeffs_df) 
  coeffs_df}) %>% bind_rows(.id = "env") 
mh_env_summ1$clust = 1

mh_env2 = lapply(env, function(env) {
  lmer(reformulate(termlabels = c(env, "(1 | src_subject_id)"), response = "resid_extmod"),
       data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long_c2, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights) |>
    summary()})
names(mh_env2) = env
mh_env_summ2 = lapply(env, function(env) {
  model_summary = mh_env2[[env]]
  coeffs = model_summary$coefficients
  row_name = env
  int_row = coeffs[rownames(coeffs) == row_name, , drop = FALSE]})
names(mh_env_summ2) = names(mh_env2)
mh_env_summ2 = lapply(mh_env_summ2, function(coeffs) {
  coeffs_df = as.data.frame(coeffs)
  coeffs_df$term = rownames(coeffs_df) 
  coeffs_df}) %>% bind_rows(.id = "env") 
mh_env_summ2$clust = 2

mh_env3 = lapply(env, function(env) {
  lmer(reformulate(termlabels = c(env, "(1 | src_subject_id)"), response = "resid_extmod"),
       data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long_c3, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights) |>
    summary()})
names(mh_env3) = env
mh_env_summ3 = lapply(env, function(env) {
  model_summary = mh_env3[[env]]
  coeffs = model_summary$coefficients
  row_name = env
  int_row = coeffs[rownames(coeffs) == row_name, , drop = FALSE]})
names(mh_env_summ3) = env
mh_env_summ3 = lapply(mh_env_summ3, function(coeffs) {
  coeffs_df = as.data.frame(coeffs)
  coeffs_df$term = rownames(coeffs_df) 
  coeffs_df}) %>% bind_rows(.id = "env") 
mh_env_summ3$clust = 3

mh_env_summ_ext = rbind(mh_env_summ1, mh_env_summ2, mh_env_summ3)
mh_env_summ_ext$padj = p.adjust(mh_env_summ_ext$`Pr(>|t|)`, method="fdr")
mh_env_summ_ext$padj = round(mh_env_summ_ext$padj, digits=3)
mh_env_summ_ext = data.frame(mh_env_summ_ext, row.names = NULL)
mh_env_summ_ext
##                                          env      Estimate  Std..Error
## 1                       Negative.life.events  0.0999233446 0.010638628
## 2                             Discrimination  0.0941702452 0.009911330
## 3                           Traumatic.events  0.0064700753 0.012469159
## 4                                  Parenting -0.1111836556 0.008288443
## 5                        Parental.monitoring -0.1123303449 0.008687647
## 6                     Parent.psychopathology  0.0665562283 0.011325270
## 7               Other.parent.psychopathology  0.0404401344 0.012257930
## 8                            Family.conflict  0.1539534330 0.008917740
## 9                            Family.cohesion -0.0479845897 0.010099066
## 10                     Family.expressiveness -0.0018711601 0.008551307
## 11            Family.int.cultural.engagement -0.0126659428 0.008277095
## 12                       Family.organization -0.0396385624 0.008850171
## 13                                  Familism -0.0093210378 0.008230326
## 14                           Prosocial.peers -0.0580488601 0.008246601
## 15                       Rule.breaking.peers  0.0773159718 0.010025937
