Test if bpm-brain association differs by groups

anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ dmn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                           Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value   Pr(>F)   
## dmn                       1.9913  1.9913     1 11053.5  3.1612 0.075434 . 
## clust                     7.5128  3.7564     2 10700.7  5.9633 0.002580 **
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 4.6990  4.6990     1  5711.3  7.4597 0.006329 **
## changeage                 0.0834  0.0834     1  2423.4  0.1324 0.715937   
## rsfmri_meanmotion         1.6523  1.6523     1  6269.2  2.6230 0.105378   
## mri_info_manufacturer     1.5126  0.7563     2     7.2  1.2006 0.354901   
## dmn:clust                 0.4634  0.2317     2 10976.4  0.3679 0.692225   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ sal * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                           Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value   Pr(>F)   
## sal                       2.4637  2.4637     1 10854.0  3.9107 0.048005 * 
## clust                     4.2178  2.1089     2 10654.8  3.3475 0.035210 * 
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 4.7250  4.7250     1  5692.0  7.5000 0.006189 **
## changeage                 0.0980  0.0980     1  2373.0  0.1556 0.693263   
## rsfmri_meanmotion         1.5597  1.5597     1  6221.7  2.4758 0.115662   
## mri_info_manufacturer     1.5708  0.7854     2     6.9  1.2466 0.344782   
## sal:clust                 0.0229  0.0115     2 10958.7  0.0182 0.981951   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                           Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value   Pr(>F)   
## fpn                       0.6845  0.6845     1 11009.3  1.0865 0.297261   
## clust                     8.2530  4.1265     2 10604.6  6.5498 0.001436 **
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 4.8371  4.8371     1  5721.8  7.6776 0.005609 **
## changeage                 0.0711  0.0711     1  2469.9  0.1128 0.736982   
## rsfmri_meanmotion         1.9605  1.9605     1  6169.1  3.1117 0.077780 . 
## mri_info_manufacturer     1.5561  0.7781     2     7.4  1.2350 0.344755   
## fpn:clust                 0.5766  0.2883     2 10939.8  0.4576 0.632797   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ dmn_sal * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                            Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## dmn_sal                    4.1059  4.1059     1 10764.5  6.5184  0.010690 *  
## clust                     25.8478 12.9239     2  9613.4 20.5176 1.283e-09 ***
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1  4.3839  4.3839     1  5720.7  6.9598  0.008359 ** 
## changeage                  0.0734  0.0734     1  2400.7  0.1166  0.732791    
## rsfmri_meanmotion          1.3363  1.3363     1  6320.9  2.1214  0.145303    
## mri_info_manufacturer      1.5927  0.7964     2     7.0  1.2643  0.339653    
## dmn_sal:clust              0.8208  0.4104     2 11228.3  0.6515  0.521280    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ dmn_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                           Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## dmn_fpn                    0.696  0.6960     1 11093.6  1.1055  0.293085    
## clust                     57.169 28.5844     2  7859.5 45.4003 < 2.2e-16 ***
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1  4.946  4.9457     1  5715.0  7.8552  0.005084 ** 
## changeage                  0.064  0.0641     1  2457.6  0.1019  0.749634    
## rsfmri_meanmotion          1.981  1.9812     1  6154.5  3.1467  0.076132 .  
## mri_info_manufacturer      1.801  0.9007     2     7.2  1.4306  0.299631    
## dmn_fpn:clust              5.514  2.7568     2 11175.5  4.3787  0.012564 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ sal_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                           Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## sal_fpn                    1.245   1.245     1 11117.0  1.9787 0.1595520    
## clust                     67.232  33.616     2  4580.3 53.4272 < 2.2e-16 ***
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1  4.766   4.766     1  5702.7  7.5745 0.0059388 ** 
## changeage                  0.083   0.083     1  2406.3  0.1323 0.7161062    
## rsfmri_meanmotion          1.925   1.925     1  6155.4  3.0601 0.0802849 .  
## mri_info_manufacturer      1.776   0.888     2     7.0  1.4115 0.3058090    
## sal_fpn:clust             11.139   5.570     2 11182.0  8.8521 0.0001441 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
p.adjust(c(0.692225, 0.981951, 0.632797, 0.521280, 0.012564, 0.0001441), method="fdr")
## [1] 0.8306700 0.9819510 0.8306700 0.8306700 0.0376920 0.0008646
anova(lmer(bpm_ext ~ dmn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                            Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## dmn                        0.0141  0.0141     1 11158.9  0.0292  0.864332    
## clust                      4.6495  2.3248     2 10932.3  4.8276  0.008023 ** 
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1  0.0351  0.0351     1  5951.7  0.0728  0.787250    
## changeage                  0.8246  0.8246     1  3101.8  1.7124  0.190769    
## rsfmri_meanmotion         17.7653 17.7653     1  6559.1 36.8917 1.319e-09 ***
## mri_info_manufacturer      1.1927  0.5963     2    11.2  1.2383  0.326567    
## dmn:clust                  0.0103  0.0051     2 11123.6  0.0107  0.989393    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(lmer(bpm_ext ~ sal * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                            Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## sal                        1.0866  1.0866     1 11057.5  2.2591   0.13286    
## clust                      2.6417  1.3208     2 10915.6  2.7461   0.06422 .  
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1  0.0536  0.0536     1  5943.7  0.1114   0.73858    
## changeage                  0.7710  0.7710     1  3056.4  1.6029   0.20559    
## rsfmri_meanmotion         16.6532 16.6532     1  6527.1 34.6225 4.201e-09 ***
## mri_info_manufacturer      1.3235  0.6617     2    10.9  1.3758   0.29325    
## sal:clust                  0.2554  0.1277     2 11123.9  0.2655   0.76681    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(lmer(bpm_ext ~ fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                            Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## fpn                        0.8268  0.8268     1 11145.7  1.7175 0.1900471    
## clust                      7.6937  3.8468     2 10861.6  7.9906 0.0003406 ***
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1  0.0440  0.0440     1  5944.5  0.0914 0.7624107    
## changeage                  0.8197  0.8197     1  3101.4  1.7026 0.1920493    
## rsfmri_meanmotion         17.4138 17.4138     1  6456.6 36.1718 1.905e-09 ***
## mri_info_manufacturer      1.1657  0.5828     2    11.2  1.2107 0.3342158    
## fpn:clust                  0.5340  0.2670     2 11109.2  0.5546 0.5743272    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(lmer(bpm_ext ~ dmn_sal * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                            Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## dmn_sal                    0.3819  0.3819     1 10858.6  0.7937    0.3730    
## clust                     20.9016 10.4508     2  9951.3 21.7192 3.872e-10 ***
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1  0.0546  0.0546     1  5948.2  0.1134    0.7364    
## changeage                  0.8373  0.8373     1  3034.0  1.7400    0.1872    
## rsfmri_meanmotion         17.0350 17.0350     1  6593.9 35.4028 2.818e-09 ***
## mri_info_manufacturer      1.1658  0.5829     2    10.8  1.2114    0.3354    
## dmn_sal:clust              0.0150  0.0075     2 11165.5  0.0156    0.9845    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(lmer(bpm_ext ~ dmn_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                           Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## dmn_fpn                    0.253  0.2531     1 11140.4  0.5257    0.4684    
## clust                     40.535 20.2673     2  8274.4 42.0994 < 2.2e-16 ***
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1  0.031  0.0307     1  5943.7  0.0637    0.8008    
## changeage                  0.842  0.8421     1  3073.4  1.7493    0.1861    
## rsfmri_meanmotion         17.672 17.6716     1  6443.9 36.7077  1.45e-09 ***
## mri_info_manufacturer      1.206  0.6032     2    10.9  1.2529    0.3238    
## dmn_fpn:clust              0.529  0.2645     2 11192.0  0.5494    0.5773    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(lmer(bpm_ext ~ sal_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                           Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## sal_fpn                    0.004  0.0045     1 11140.5  0.0093    0.9231    
## clust                     61.373 30.6863     2  5041.8 63.7880 < 2.2e-16 ***
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1  0.043  0.0433     1  5954.5  0.0900    0.7642    
## changeage                  0.836  0.8356     1  3097.5  1.7370    0.1876    
## rsfmri_meanmotion         17.871 17.8708     1  6477.6 37.1481 1.158e-09 ***
## mri_info_manufacturer      1.182  0.5909     2    11.0  1.2283    0.3301    
## sal_fpn:clust              1.468  0.7340     2 11185.5  1.5259    0.2175    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
p.adjust(c(0.989393, 0.76681, 0.5743272, 0.9845, 0.5773, 0.2175), method="fdr")
## [1] 0.989393 0.989393 0.989393 0.989393 0.989393 0.989393
networks = c( "dmn_fpn", "sal_fpn")

moddmnfpn = (lmer(bpm_int ~ dmn_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
modsalfpn = (lmer(bpm_int ~ sal_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))

test(emtrends(moddmnfpn, pairwise ~ clust, var = "dmn_fpn", adjust="fdr"))
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 11256' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 11256)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 11256' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 11256)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emtrends
##  clust dmn_fpn.trend    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1             1.260 0.567 Inf   2.224  0.0262
##  2            -2.045 1.110 Inf  -1.847  0.0647
##  3            -0.933 1.050 Inf  -0.885  0.3759
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2     3.30 1.24 Inf   2.661  0.0234
##  clust1 - clust3     2.19 1.19 Inf   1.837  0.0994
##  clust2 - clust3    -1.11 1.53 Inf  -0.728  0.4663
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
test(emtrends(modsalfpn, pairwise ~ clust, var = "sal_fpn", adjust="fdr"))
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 11253' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 11253)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 11253' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 11253)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emtrends
##  clust sal_fpn.trend    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1             0.746 0.569 Inf   1.312  0.1895
##  2             3.996 1.120 Inf   3.563  0.0004
##  3            -2.431 1.040 Inf  -2.328  0.0199
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2    -3.25 1.25 Inf  -2.592  0.0095
##  clust1 - clust3     3.18 1.19 Inf   2.679  0.0095
##  clust2 - clust3     6.43 1.53 Inf   4.203  0.0001
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests

Exploratory differences by dev timing

anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ dmn_fpn * clust * age + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                           Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## dmn_fpn                   1.0017  1.0017     1 9845.5  1.5930 0.2069314    
## clust                     8.1056  4.0528     2 9781.3  6.4449 0.0015954 ** 
## age                       0.4097  0.4097     1 8888.4  0.6516 0.4195756    
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 8.9477  8.9477     1 7676.7 14.2289 0.0001631 ***
## changeage                 0.1496  0.1496     1 2455.8  0.2379 0.6257722    
## rsfmri_meanmotion         1.5729  1.5729     1 6184.4  2.5012 0.1138085    
## mri_info_manufacturer     1.8242  0.9121     2    7.3  1.4505 0.2950591    
## dmn_fpn:clust             4.1142  2.0571     2 9843.3  3.2712 0.0380003 *  
## dmn_fpn:age               0.8753  0.8753     1 9744.4  1.3919 0.2381080    
## clust:age                 5.9470  2.9735     2 9670.1  4.7286 0.0088594 ** 
## dmn_fpn:clust:age         3.5378  1.7689     2 9741.3  2.8129 0.0600775 .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ sal_fpn * clust * age + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                           Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value   Pr(>F)    
## sal_fpn                   0.2070  0.2070     1 9951.8  0.3295 0.565991    
## clust                     5.4580  2.7290     2 9552.3  4.3441 0.013008 *  
## age                       5.0573  5.0573     1 7725.3  8.0505 0.004561 ** 
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 8.9319  8.9319     1 7651.7 14.2183 0.000164 ***
## changeage                 0.1739  0.1739     1 2414.6  0.2769 0.598787    
## rsfmri_meanmotion         1.5013  1.5013     1 6186.7  2.3898 0.122181    
## mri_info_manufacturer     1.8260  0.9130     2    7.1  1.4534 0.296083    
## sal_fpn:clust             3.7937  1.8969     2 9963.9  3.0195 0.048869 *  
## sal_fpn:age               0.3438  0.3438     1 9945.2  0.5472 0.459477    
## clust:age                 5.5299  2.7650     2 9454.7  4.4014 0.012285 *  
## sal_fpn:clust:age         5.1892  2.5946     2 9948.8  4.1302 0.016107 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
moddmnfpn_age = (lmer(bpm_int ~ dmn_fpn * clust * age + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
modsalfpn_age = (lmer(bpm_int ~ sal_fpn * clust * age + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))

agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% dplyr::summarize(mean = mean(age, na.rm=T), sd = sd(age, na.rm=T))
##       mean       sd
## 1 11.65669 1.070528
test(emtrends(moddmnfpn_age, pairwise ~ clust, var = "dmn_fpn", adjust="fdr"))
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 11256' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 11256)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 11256' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 11256)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
## $emtrends
##  clust dmn_fpn.trend    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1              1.33 0.569 Inf   2.347  0.0189
##  2             -1.86 1.120 Inf  -1.669  0.0951
##  3             -1.05 1.060 Inf  -0.997  0.3190
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2     3.20 1.25 Inf   2.556  0.0318
##  clust1 - clust3     2.39 1.20 Inf   1.994  0.0692
##  clust2 - clust3    -0.81 1.53 Inf  -0.528  0.5975
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
test(emtrends(modsalfpn_age, pairwise ~ clust, var = "sal_fpn", adjust="fdr"))
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 11253' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 11253)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 11253' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 11253)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
## $emtrends
##  clust sal_fpn.trend    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1             0.845 0.571 Inf   1.479  0.1391
##  2             4.052 1.120 Inf   3.614  0.0003
##  3            -2.342 1.050 Inf  -2.238  0.0252
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2    -3.21 1.25 Inf  -2.556  0.0106
##  clust1 - clust3     3.19 1.19 Inf   2.680  0.0106
##  clust2 - clust3     6.39 1.53 Inf   4.178  0.0001
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
networks = c("dmn_fpn", "sal_fpn")

bpmbrain_int_age = lapply(networks, function(net) {
  lmer(reformulate(termlabels = c(paste0(net, "*clust*age"), "age_baseline_year_1_arm_1", "changeage", 
                                  "(1 | src_subject_id)", "(1 | site_id_l)",
                                  "rsfmri_meanmotion"), response = "bpm_int"),
       data = agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights) |>
    summary()})
names(bpmbrain_int_age) = networks

bpmbrain_int_age_summ1 = lapply(networks, function(net) {
  model_summary = bpmbrain_int_age[[net]]
  coeffs = model_summary$coefficients
  row_name = paste0(net, ":clust2:age")
  int_row = coeffs[rownames(coeffs) == row_name, , drop = FALSE]})
names(bpmbrain_int_age_summ1) = names(bpmbrain_int_age)
bpmbrain_int_age_summ1 = lapply(bpmbrain_int_age_summ1, function(coeffs) {
  coeffs_df = as.data.frame(coeffs)
  coeffs_df$term = rownames(coeffs_df) 
  coeffs_df}) %>% bind_rows(.id = "network") 

bpmbrain_int_age_summ2 = lapply(networks, function(net) {
  model_summary = bpmbrain_int_age[[net]]
  coeffs = model_summary$coefficients
  row_name = paste0(net, ":clust3:age")
  int_row = coeffs[rownames(coeffs) == row_name, , drop = FALSE]})
names(bpmbrain_int_age_summ2) = names(bpmbrain_int_age)
bpmbrain_int_age_summ2 = lapply(bpmbrain_int_age_summ2, function(coeffs) {
  coeffs_df = as.data.frame(coeffs)
  coeffs_df$term = rownames(coeffs_df) 
  coeffs_df}) %>% bind_rows(.id = "network") 

bpmbrain_int_age2 = lapply(networks, function(net) {
  lmer(reformulate(termlabels = c(paste0(net, "*relevel(clust,ref=2)*age"), "age_baseline_year_1_arm_1", "changeage", 
                                  "(1 | src_subject_id)", "(1 | site_id_l)", "mri_info_manufacturer",
                                  "rsfmri_meanmotion"), response = "bpm_int"),
       data = agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights) |>
    summary()})
names(bpmbrain_int_age2) = networks
bpmbrain_int_age_summ3 = lapply(networks, function(net) {
  model_summary = bpmbrain_int_age2[[net]]
  coeffs = model_summary$coefficients
  row_name = paste0(net, ":relevel(clust, ref = 2)3:age")
  int_row = coeffs[rownames(coeffs) == row_name, , drop = FALSE]})
names(bpmbrain_int_age_summ3) = names(bpmbrain_int_age2)
bpmbrain_int_age_summ3 = lapply(bpmbrain_int_age_summ3, function(coeffs) {
  coeffs_df = as.data.frame(coeffs)
  coeffs_df$term = rownames(coeffs_df) 
  coeffs_df}) %>% bind_rows(.id = "network") 

bpmbrain_int_age_summ = rbind(bpmbrain_int_age_summ1, bpmbrain_int_age_summ2, bpmbrain_int_age_summ3)
bpmbrain_int_age_summ$padj = p.adjust(bpmbrain_int_age_summ$`Pr(>|t|)`, method="fdr")
bpmbrain_int_age_summ = data.frame(bpmbrain_int_age_summ, row.names = NULL)
bpmbrain_int_age_summ
##   network   Estimate Std..Error        df    t.value    Pr...t..
## 1 dmn_fpn  0.5650529   1.070997  9790.213  0.5275951 0.597792344
## 2 sal_fpn  2.6979725   1.105684 10034.021  2.4400936 0.014700646
## 3 dmn_fpn  2.4744472   1.042793  9687.848  2.3729027 0.017668317
## 4 sal_fpn -0.9873120   1.051315  9862.162 -0.9391214 0.347691439
## 5 dmn_fpn  1.9074392   1.322317  9750.597  1.4424976 0.149194156
## 6 sal_fpn -3.6940489   1.348604  9954.945 -2.7391657 0.006170496
##                                   term       padj
## 1                   dmn_fpn:clust2:age 0.59779234
## 2                   sal_fpn:clust2:age 0.03533663
## 3                   dmn_fpn:clust3:age 0.03533663
## 4                   sal_fpn:clust3:age 0.41722973
## 5 dmn_fpn:relevel(clust, ref = 2)3:age 0.22379123
## 6 sal_fpn:relevel(clust, ref = 2)3:age 0.03533663

Sensitivity analysis with puberty and sex

# baseline mental health #
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + (1 | site_id_l) + pub_base + sex, data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% filter(timepoint == "6_month_follow_up"), weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                              Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## clust                       103.080  51.540     2 6981.5 42.7649 < 2.2e-16 ***
## age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1  41.881  41.881     1 7480.1 34.7502 3.911e-09 ***
## pub_base                     22.283  22.283     1 7616.4 18.4892 1.730e-05 ***
## sex                           9.647   9.647     1 7646.5  8.0049  0.004677 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
base_supp1 = (lmer(bpm_int ~ clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + (1 | site_id_l) + pub_base + sex, data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% filter(timepoint == "6_month_follow_up"), weights = weights))
emmeans(base_supp1, pairwise ~ clust, adjust="fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 7653' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 7653)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 7653' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 7653)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emmeans
##  clust emmean     SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  1       1.58 0.0433 Inf      1.50      1.67
##  2       2.08 0.0646 Inf      1.95      2.20
##  3       1.99 0.0611 Inf      1.87      2.11
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2  -0.4937 0.0630 Inf  -7.830  <.0001
##  clust1 - clust3  -0.4072 0.0605 Inf  -6.730  <.0001
##  clust2 - clust3   0.0864 0.0756 Inf   1.143  0.2531
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
anova(lmer(bpm_ext ~ clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + (1 | site_id_l) + pub_base + sex, data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% filter(timepoint == "6_month_follow_up"), weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                              Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## clust                       130.810  65.405     2 7013.6 60.0822 < 2.2e-16 ***
## age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1  11.484  11.484     1 7496.6 10.5494  0.001167 ** 
## pub_base                      5.762   5.762     1 7645.3  5.2927  0.021442 *  
## sex                          60.364  60.364     1 7671.5 55.4517 1.062e-13 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
base_supp2 = (lmer(bpm_ext ~ clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + (1 | site_id_l) + pub_base + sex, data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% filter(timepoint == "6_month_follow_up"), weights = weights))
emmeans(base_supp2, pairwise ~ clust, adjust="fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 7679' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 7679)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 7679' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 7679)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emmeans
##  clust emmean     SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  1       1.70 0.0397 Inf      1.62      1.78
##  2       2.34 0.0605 Inf      2.22      2.46
##  3       2.00 0.0568 Inf      1.88      2.11
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2   -0.639 0.0599 Inf -10.663  <.0001
##  clust1 - clust3   -0.294 0.0571 Inf  -5.141  <.0001
##  clust2 - clust3    0.345 0.0716 Inf   4.815  <.0001
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
# change over time mental health #
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l) + pub_base + sex, agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                              Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF DenDF  F value    Pr(>F)    
## age                         148.276 148.276     1 35922 184.9102 < 2.2e-16 ***
## I(age^2)                    173.771 173.771     1 35958 216.7042 < 2.2e-16 ***
## clust                        12.102   6.051     2 43157   7.5462 0.0005288 ***
## age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1  36.163  36.163     1 16527  45.0983 1.935e-11 ***
## changeage                    12.172  12.172     1 34791  15.1796 9.794e-05 ***
## pub_base                     44.249  44.249     1 12619  55.1820 1.169e-13 ***
## sex                          62.357  62.357     1 12978  77.7630 < 2.2e-16 ***
## I(age^2):clust               16.146   8.073     2 41025  10.0674 4.254e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
supp1 = (lmer(bpm_int ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l) + pub_base + sex, agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
emtrends(supp1, pairwise ~ clust, var = "age", adjust="fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 43581' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 43581)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 43581' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 43581)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emtrends
##  clust age.trend     SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  1         0.150 0.0117 Inf    0.1272     0.173
##  2         0.228 0.0213 Inf    0.1863     0.270
##  3         0.103 0.0196 Inf    0.0642     0.141
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2  -0.0780 0.0233 Inf  -3.346  0.0012
##  clust1 - clust3   0.0475 0.0218 Inf   2.172  0.0298
##  clust2 - clust3   0.1254 0.0282 Inf   4.451  <.0001
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
anova(lmer(bpm_ext ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l) + pub_base + sex, agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                             Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## age                         33.165  33.165     1 35030 57.5164 3.435e-14 ***
## I(age^2)                    41.306  41.306     1 35059 71.6334 < 2.2e-16 ***
## clust                        7.966   3.983     2 42516  6.9072 0.0010017 ** 
## age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 10.320  10.320     1 16782 17.8975 2.344e-05 ***
## changeage                   33.841  33.841     1 34111 58.6880 1.896e-14 ***
## pub_base                     8.633   8.633     1 13380 14.9713 0.0001097 ***
## sex                         18.982  18.982     1 13765 32.9187 9.812e-09 ***
## I(age^2):clust               4.117   2.059     2 39612  3.5703 0.0281553 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
supp2 = (lmer(bpm_ext ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l) + pub_base + sex, agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
test(emtrends(supp2, pairwise ~ clust, var = "age", adjust="fdr"))
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 43331' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 43331)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 43331' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 43331)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emtrends
##  clust age.trend      SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1        0.0805 0.00993 Inf   8.111  <.0001
##  2        0.1279 0.01820 Inf   7.041  <.0001
##  3        0.1128 0.01670 Inf   6.739  <.0001
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2  -0.0474 0.0199 Inf  -2.374  0.0528
##  clust1 - clust3  -0.0323 0.0187 Inf  -1.723  0.1272
##  clust2 - clust3   0.0151 0.0241 Inf   0.625  0.5317
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
# brain-beh among clusters #
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ sal_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer + pub_base + sex, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                           Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## sal_fpn                    1.159  1.1587     1 10841.3  1.8630 0.1723072    
## clust                     57.277 28.6384     2  4541.7 46.0457 < 2.2e-16 ***
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1  6.793  6.7931     1  5468.4 10.9222 0.0009563 ***
## changeage                  0.073  0.0730     1  2289.9  0.1173 0.7319707    
## rsfmri_meanmotion          2.617  2.6170     1  5913.1  4.2077 0.0402837 *  
## mri_info_manufacturer      2.029  1.0147     2     7.1  1.6315 0.2614440    
## pub_base                  13.837 13.8373     1  5663.8 22.2480 2.454e-06 ***
## sex                        2.358  2.3579     1  6029.2  3.7911 0.0515715 .  
## sal_fpn:clust             11.314  5.6572     2 10906.6  9.0958 0.0001130 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
supp3 = (lmer(bpm_int ~ sal_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer + pub_base + sex, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
emtrends(supp3, pairwise ~ clust, var = "sal_fpn", adjust="fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 11000' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 11000)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 11000' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 11000)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emtrends
##  clust sal_fpn.trend   SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  1             0.739 0.57 Inf    -0.378     1.856
##  2             4.056 1.13 Inf     1.839     6.272
##  3            -2.530 1.06 Inf    -4.604    -0.455
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer, sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2    -3.32 1.26 Inf  -2.627  0.0086
##  clust1 - clust3     3.27 1.20 Inf   2.727  0.0086
##  clust2 - clust3     6.59 1.55 Inf   4.260  0.0001
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer, sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ dmn_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer + pub_base + sex, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                           Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## dmn_fpn                    0.515  0.5146     1 10804.1  0.8266 0.3632720    
## clust                     47.967 23.9837     2  7698.2 38.5295 < 2.2e-16 ***
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1  7.023  7.0228     1  5480.5 11.2820 0.0007879 ***
## changeage                  0.053  0.0526     1  2332.6  0.0845 0.7712894    
## rsfmri_meanmotion          2.667  2.6669     1  5916.1  4.2844 0.0385067 *  
## mri_info_manufacturer      2.033  1.0165     2     7.3  1.6330 0.2592083    
## pub_base                  13.954 13.9536     1  5673.4 22.4162 2.248e-06 ***
## sex                        2.273  2.2733     1  6024.0  3.6520 0.0560481 .  
## dmn_fpn:clust              4.572  2.2858     2 10893.8  3.6721 0.0254542 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
supp4 = (lmer(bpm_int ~ dmn_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer + pub_base + sex, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
emtrends(supp4, pairwise ~ clust, var = "dmn_fpn", adjust="fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 11003' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 11003)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 11003' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 11003)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emtrends
##  clust dmn_fpn.trend    SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  1              1.14 0.569 Inf    0.0242     2.255
##  2             -2.03 1.110 Inf   -4.2026     0.138
##  3             -0.61 1.080 Inf   -2.7298     1.509
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer, sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2     3.17 1.24 Inf   2.551  0.0322
##  clust1 - clust3     1.75 1.22 Inf   1.435  0.2271
##  clust2 - clust3    -1.42 1.55 Inf  -0.920  0.3578
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer, sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests

Sensitivity analyses with random sampling of sib

Create dfs

set.seed(123) 
agnes_res_env_sample = agnes_res_env %>%
  group_by(rel_family_id) %>% 
  slice_sample(n = 1) %>% 
  ungroup() 

# create long version of bpm
agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long = 
  agnes_res_env_sample %>% 
  select(src_subject_id, clust, 
         bpm_y_scr_internal_r_6_month_follow_up_arm_1, bpm_y_scr_internal_r_1_year_follow_up_y_arm_1, 
         bpm_y_scr_internal_r_18_month_follow_up_arm_1, bpm_y_scr_internal_r_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1,
         bpm_y_scr_internal_r_30_month_follow_up_arm_1, bpm_y_scr_internal_r_3_year_follow_up_y_arm_1, 
         rsfmri_meanmotion, mri_info_manufacturer, site_id_l, rel_family_id, sex, weights, 
         pub_base, demo_sex_v2) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("bpm_y_scr"), names_pattern = "bpm_y_scr_internal_r_(.*)_arm_1",
               names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "bpm_int") %>% 
  cbind((agnes_res_env_sample %>% select(src_subject_id, 
                                  bpm_y_scr_external_r_6_month_follow_up_arm_1, 
                                  bpm_y_scr_external_r_1_year_follow_up_y_arm_1, 
                                  bpm_y_scr_external_r_18_month_follow_up_arm_1, 
                                  bpm_y_scr_external_r_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1,
                                  bpm_y_scr_external_r_30_month_follow_up_arm_1, 
                                  bpm_y_scr_external_r_3_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
           pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("bpm_y_scr"), names_pattern = "bpm_y_scr_external_r_(.*)_arm_1",
                        names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "bpm_ext") %>% select(bpm_ext)), 
        (agnes_res_env_sample %>% select(src_subject_id, 
                                  bpm_y_scr_totalprob_r_6_month_follow_up_arm_1, 
                                  bpm_y_scr_totalprob_r_1_year_follow_up_y_arm_1, 
                                  bpm_y_scr_totalprob_r_18_month_follow_up_arm_1, 
                                  bpm_y_scr_totalprob_r_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1,
                                  bpm_y_scr_totalprob_r_30_month_follow_up_arm_1,
                                  bpm_y_scr_totalprob_r_3_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
           pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("bpm_y_scr"), names_pattern = "bpm_y_scr_total_r_(.*)_arm_1",
                        names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "bpm_tot")) %>% select(bpm_tot)) %>% 
  mutate(timepoint = as.factor(timepoint))
agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long = left_join(agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long,
                                   age %>% pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("age_"), names_pattern = "age_(.*)_arm_1",
                                                        names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "age") %>% 
                                     select(src_subject_id, timepoint, age))
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, timepoint)`
agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long = left_join(agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long, agnes_res_env_sample %>% 
                                     select(src_subject_id, age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1))
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id)`
# create a new df with changeage only
changeage = age %>% pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("changeage_"), names_pattern = "changeage_(.*)",
                                 names_to = "change_timepoint", values_to = "changeage") %>%
  select(src_subject_id, change_timepoint, changeage) %>% filter(str_starts(change_timepoint, "from6mo_to"))
changeage = changeage %>% mutate(timepoint = if_else(change_timepoint == "from6mo_to1yr", "1_year_follow_up_y",
                                                     if_else(change_timepoint == "from1yr_to18mo", "18_month_follow_up",
                                                             if_else(change_timepoint == "from18mo_to2yr", "2_year_follow_up_y",
                                                                     if_else(change_timepoint == "from2yr_to30mo", "30_month_follow_up",
                                                                             if_else(change_timepoint == "from30mo_to3yr", "3_year_follow_up_y",
                                                                                     NA_character_))))))
agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long = left_join(agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long, changeage)
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, timepoint)`
agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long = agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long %>% mutate(changeage = if_else(!is.na(age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1) & is.na(change_timepoint), 0, changeage))

# create long version of the brain
agnes_res_env_sample_brain_long = 
  agnes_res_env_sample %>% 
  select(src_subject_id, clust, 
         dmn_dmn_baseline_year_1_arm_1, dmn_dmn_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1, 
         rsfmri_meanmotion, mri_info_manufacturer, site_id_l, rel_family_id, sex, weights, 
         pub_base, demo_sex_v2) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = ends_with("arm_1"), names_pattern = "dmn_dmn_(.*)_arm_1",
               names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "dmn") %>%
  cbind(agnes_res_env_sample %>% 
          select(src_subject_id, sal_sal_baseline_year_1_arm_1, sal_sal_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
          pivot_longer(cols = ends_with("arm_1"), names_pattern = "sal_sal_(.*)_arm_1",
                       names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "sal") %>% select(sal)) %>% 
  cbind(agnes_res_env_sample %>% 
          select(src_subject_id, fpn_fpn_baseline_year_1_arm_1, fpn_fpn_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
          pivot_longer(cols = ends_with("arm_1"), names_pattern = "fpn_fpn_(.*)_arm_1",
                       names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "fpn") %>% select(fpn)) %>% 
  cbind(agnes_res_env_sample %>% 
          select(src_subject_id, dmn_sal_baseline_year_1_arm_1, dmn_sal_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
          pivot_longer(cols = ends_with("arm_1"), names_pattern = "dmn_sal_(.*)_arm_1",
                       names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "dmn_sal") %>% select(dmn_sal)) %>% 
  cbind(agnes_res_env_sample %>% 
          select(src_subject_id, dmn_fpn_baseline_year_1_arm_1, dmn_fpn_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
          pivot_longer(cols = ends_with("arm_1"), names_pattern = "dmn_fpn_(.*)_arm_1",
                       names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "dmn_fpn") %>% select(dmn_fpn)) %>% 
  cbind(agnes_res_env_sample %>% 
          select(src_subject_id, sal_fpn_baseline_year_1_arm_1, sal_fpn_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
          pivot_longer(cols = ends_with("arm_1"), names_pattern = "sal_fpn_(.*)_arm_1",
                       names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "sal_fpn") %>% select(sal_fpn)) %>% 
  mutate(timepoint = as.factor(timepoint))
agnes_res_env_sample_brain_long = left_join(agnes_res_env_sample_brain_long, agnes_res_env_sample %>% 
                                       select(src_subject_id, age_baseline_year_1_arm_1))
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id)`
agnes_res_env_sample_brain_long = left_join(agnes_res_env_sample_brain_long, age %>%
                                       pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("age_"), names_pattern = "age_(.*)_arm_1",
                                                    names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "age") %>%
                                       filter(timepoint=="baseline_year_1" | timepoint=="2_year_follow_up_y") %>%
                                       select(src_subject_id, timepoint, age))
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, timepoint)`
agnes_res_env_sample_brain_long = left_join(agnes_res_env_sample_brain_long,
                                     age %>% pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("changeage_"), names_pattern = "changeage_(.*)",
                                                          names_to = "change_timepoint", values_to = "changeage") %>% 
                                       select(src_subject_id, change_timepoint, changeage) %>%
                                       filter(change_timepoint == "frombase_to2yr"), by="src_subject_id") 

# create a brainbpm long version
agnes_res_env_sample_brainbpm_long = 
  left_join(agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long %>% 
              mutate(timepoint = case_when(
                timepoint == "6_month_follow_up" ~ "baseline_year_1", TRUE ~ timepoint)) %>% 
              select(-c(change_timepoint, changeage)), 
            agnes_res_env_sample_brain_long %>% select(-c(age)))
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, clust, rsfmri_meanmotion,
## mri_info_manufacturer, site_id_l, rel_family_id, sex, weights, pub_base,
## demo_sex_v2, timepoint)`

Test models using randomly sampled datasets

# baseline mental health #
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + (1 | site_id_l), data = agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long %>% filter(timepoint == "6_month_follow_up"), weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                              Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## clust                       120.835  60.418     2 6095.3   49.18 < 2.2e-16 ***
## age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1  30.087  30.087     1 6585.9   24.49 7.654e-07 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
base_supp1_samp = (lmer(bpm_int ~ clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + (1 | site_id_l), data = agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long %>% filter(timepoint == "6_month_follow_up"), weights = weights))
emmeans(base_supp1_samp, pairwise ~ clust, adjust="fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 6642' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 6642)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 6642' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 6642)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emmeans
##  clust emmean     SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  1       1.58 0.0464 Inf      1.49      1.67
##  2       2.14 0.0694 Inf      2.00      2.27
##  3       2.05 0.0632 Inf      1.93      2.17
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2  -0.5573 0.0681 Inf  -8.183  <.0001
##  clust1 - clust3  -0.4697 0.0627 Inf  -7.489  <.0001
##  clust2 - clust3   0.0876 0.0801 Inf   1.094  0.2741
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
anova(lmer(bpm_ext ~ clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + (1 | site_id_l), data = agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long %>% filter(timepoint == "6_month_follow_up"), weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                              Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## clust                       137.273  68.636     2 6105.4 61.7503 < 2.2e-16 ***
## age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1   8.333   8.333     1 6609.1  7.4965  0.006198 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
base_supp2_samp = (lmer(bpm_ext ~ clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + (1 | site_id_l), data = agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long %>% filter(timepoint == "6_month_follow_up"), weights = weights))
emmeans(base_supp2_samp, pairwise ~ clust, adjust="fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 6667' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 6667)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 6667' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 6667)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emmeans
##  clust emmean     SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  1       1.69 0.0430 Inf      1.60      1.77
##  2       2.37 0.0653 Inf      2.24      2.50
##  3       2.04 0.0591 Inf      1.93      2.16
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2   -0.684 0.0648 Inf -10.563  <.0001
##  clust1 - clust3   -0.357 0.0593 Inf  -6.015  <.0001
##  clust2 - clust3    0.327 0.0760 Inf   4.310  <.0001
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
# change over time mental health #
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                              Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF DenDF  F value    Pr(>F)    
## age                         128.334 128.334     1 30989 155.0816 < 2.2e-16 ***
## I(age^2)                    149.065 149.065     1 31020 180.1322 < 2.2e-16 ***
## clust                        17.846   8.923     2 37290  10.7826 2.082e-05 ***
## age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1  16.478  16.478     1 14460  19.9126 8.168e-06 ***
## changeage                    11.252  11.252     1 30026  13.5969  0.000227 ***
## I(age^2):clust                6.434   3.217     2 35396   3.8877  0.020502 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
supp1_samp = (lmer(bpm_int ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
emtrends(supp1_samp, pairwise ~ clust, var = "age", adjust="fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 37730' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 37730)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 37730' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 37730)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emtrends
##  clust age.trend     SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  1         0.152 0.0128 Inf    0.1267     0.177
##  2         0.188 0.0231 Inf    0.1426     0.233
##  3         0.106 0.0204 Inf    0.0656     0.146
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2  -0.0362 0.0254 Inf  -1.423  0.1548
##  clust1 - clust3   0.0461 0.0231 Inf   2.001  0.0681
##  clust2 - clust3   0.0823 0.0301 Inf   2.736  0.0186
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
anova(lmer(bpm_ext ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                             Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## age                         31.465  31.465     1 30344 53.6258 2.486e-13 ***
## I(age^2)                    37.882  37.882     1 30370 64.5616 9.693e-16 ***
## clust                       15.881   7.941     2 36788 13.5331 1.333e-06 ***
## age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1  3.600   3.600     1 14653  6.1358   0.01326 *  
## changeage                   26.615  26.615     1 29566 45.3590 1.670e-11 ***
## I(age^2):clust               0.738   0.369     2 34270  0.6290   0.53314    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
supp2_samp = (lmer(bpm_ext ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
test(emtrends(supp2_samp, pairwise ~ clust, var = "age", adjust="fdr"))
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 37535' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 37535)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 37535' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 37535)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emtrends
##  clust age.trend     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1        0.0812 0.0108 Inf   7.510  <.0001
##  2        0.0948 0.0196 Inf   4.829  <.0001
##  3        0.1017 0.0173 Inf   5.869  <.0001
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2 -0.01360 0.0216 Inf  -0.629  0.7847
##  clust1 - clust3 -0.02058 0.0196 Inf  -1.049  0.7847
##  clust2 - clust3 -0.00698 0.0256 Inf  -0.273  0.7847
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
# brain-beh among clusters #
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ sal_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_sample_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                           Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## sal_fpn                    0.983  0.9832     1 9435.4  1.5238   0.21708    
## clust                     62.750 31.3748     2 4127.2 48.6228 < 2.2e-16 ***
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1  3.862  3.8617     1 4909.8  5.9847   0.01447 *  
## changeage                  0.053  0.0528     1 2375.9  0.0818   0.77486    
## rsfmri_meanmotion          1.069  1.0694     1 5292.4  1.6573   0.19803    
## mri_info_manufacturer      1.384  0.6918     2    8.9  1.0721   0.38251    
## sal_fpn:clust             12.103  6.0516     2 9471.2  9.3783 8.532e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
supp3_samp = (lmer(bpm_int ~ sal_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_sample_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
emtrends(supp3_samp, pairwise ~ clust, var = "sal_fpn", adjust="fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 9508' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 9508)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 9508' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 9508)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emtrends
##  clust sal_fpn.trend    SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  1             0.581 0.636 Inf    -0.666     1.827
##  2             4.405 1.230 Inf     1.985     6.825
##  3            -2.777 1.110 Inf    -4.960    -0.594
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2    -3.82 1.38 Inf  -2.762  0.0086
##  clust1 - clust3     3.36 1.28 Inf   2.626  0.0086
##  clust2 - clust3     7.18 1.66 Inf   4.329  <.0001
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ dmn_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_sample_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                           Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value  Pr(>F)    
## dmn_fpn                    0.445  0.4445     1 9392.9  0.6883 0.40678    
## clust                     51.003 25.5014     2 6760.9 39.4828 < 2e-16 ***
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1  4.052  4.0518     1 4907.8  6.2733 0.01229 *  
## changeage                  0.035  0.0352     1 2406.6  0.0545 0.81544    
## rsfmri_meanmotion          1.067  1.0675     1 5272.8  1.6527 0.19865    
## mri_info_manufacturer      1.442  0.7209     2    9.2  1.1161 0.36816    
## dmn_fpn:clust              4.480  2.2400     2 9435.0  3.4681 0.03122 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
supp4_samp = (lmer(bpm_int ~ dmn_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_sample_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
emtrends(supp4_samp, pairwise ~ clust, var = "dmn_fpn", adjust="fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 9511' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 9511)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 9511' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 9511)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emtrends
##  clust dmn_fpn.trend    SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  1              1.34 0.632 Inf    0.0972     2.574
##  2             -1.38 1.220 Inf   -3.7729     1.006
##  3             -1.44 1.140 Inf   -3.6666     0.796
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2   2.7191 1.37 Inf   1.982  0.0712
##  clust1 - clust3   2.7709 1.30 Inf   2.132  0.0712
##  clust2 - clust3   0.0517 1.67 Inf   0.031  0.9752
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests

Plot/Visualize results

Demographic table

agnes_res_env %>% group_by(clust) %>% dplyr::summarize(median(demo_comb_income_v2, na.rm = T))
## # A tibble: 3 × 2
##   clust `median(demo_comb_income_v2, na.rm = T)`
##   <fct>                                    <int>
## 1 1                                            9
## 2 2                                            8
## 3 3                                            6
agnes_res_env = agnes_res_env %>% 
  mutate(demo_sex_v2 = as.factor(demo_sex_v2), 
         race_ethnicity = as.factor(race_ethnicity), 
         demo_comb_income_v2 = as.factor(demo_comb_income_v2))
label(agnes_res_env$demo_sex_v2) = "Gender"
label(agnes_res_env$age_baseline_year_1_arm_1) = "Age"
label(agnes_res_env$race_ethnicity) = "Race/Ethnicity"
label(agnes_res_env$demo_comb_income_v2) = "Household Income"
label(agnes_res_env$demo_prnt_ed_v2) = "Parent Education"
agnes_res_env$clust_num = as.factor(as.numeric(agnes_res_env$clust))
levels(agnes_res_env$clust_num) = c("Low risk (66%)", "Trauma, parental and\nfamily risk (16%)", "Disadvantage 18%)")

table1(~ demo_sex_v2 + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + race_ethnicity + 
         demo_comb_income_v2 + demo_prnt_ed_v2 | clust_num, 
       data=agnes_res_env)
Low risk (66%)
(N=5329)		 Trauma, parental and family risk (16%)
(N=1279)		 Disadvantage 18%)
(N=1470)		 Overall
(N=8078)

1 3704 (69.5%) 787 (61.5%) 372 (25.3%) 4863 (60.2%)

1 23 (0.4%) 14 (1.1%) 76 (5.2%) 113 (1.4%)
2 39 (0.7%) 18 (1.4%) 94 (6.4%) 151 (1.9%)
3 33 (0.6%) 24 (1.9%) 50 (3.4%) 107 (1.3%)

5 119 (2.2%) 78 (6.1%) 145 (9.9%) 342 (4.2%)
6 249 (4.7%) 116 (9.1%) 184 (12.5%) 549 (6.8%)
7 606 (11.4%) 209 (16.3%) 244 (16.6%) 1059 (13.1%)

Mean (SD) 17.6 (2.10) 16.9 (2.22) 15.2 (3.01) 17.1 (2.48)
Median [Min, Max] 18.0 [1.00, 21.0] 18.0 [6.00, 21.0] 15.0 [1.00, 21.0] 18.0 [1.00, 21.0]
Missing 5 (0.1%) 2 (0.2%) 3 (0.2%) 10 (0.1%) 
# compare demo
summary(aov(age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 ~ clust, agnes_res_env))
##               Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value Pr(>F)
## clust          2    1.7  0.8748   2.237  0.107
## Residuals   8075 3158.2  0.3911
summary(aov(demo_prnt_ed_v2 ~ clust, agnes_res_env))
##               Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value Pr(>F)    
## clust          2   6700    3350   627.9 <2e-16 ***
## Residuals   8065  43033       5                   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 10 observations deleted due to missingness
chisq.test(table(agnes_res_env$demo_sex_v2, agnes_res_env$clust))
## Warning in chisq.test(table(agnes_res_env$demo_sex_v2, agnes_res_env$clust)):
## Chi-squared approximation may be incorrect
## 
##  Pearson's Chi-squared test
## 
## data:  table(agnes_res_env$demo_sex_v2, agnes_res_env$clust)
## X-squared = 7.0466, df = 4, p-value = 0.1334
chisq.test(table(agnes_res_env$race_ethnicity, agnes_res_env$clust))
## 
##  Pearson's Chi-squared test
## 
## data:  table(agnes_res_env$race_ethnicity, agnes_res_env$clust)
## X-squared = 1481.5, df = 8, p-value < 2.2e-16
chisq.test(table(agnes_res_env$demo_comb_income_v2, agnes_res_env$clust))
## 
##  Pearson's Chi-squared test
## 
## data:  table(agnes_res_env$demo_comb_income_v2, agnes_res_env$clust)
## X-squared = 1580.9, df = 18, p-value < 2.2e-16
agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% group_by(timepoint) %>% dplyr::summarize(mean(age, na.rm=T), sd(age, na.rm=T))
## # A tibble: 6 × 3
##   timepoint          `mean(age, na.rm = T)` `sd(age, na.rm = T)`
##   <chr>                               <dbl>                <dbl>
## 1 18_month_follow_up                   11.4                0.636
## 2 1_year_follow_up_y                   10.9                0.642
## 3 2_year_follow_up_y                   12.0                0.661
## 4 30_month_follow_up                   12.4                0.639
## 5 3_year_follow_up_y                   12.9                0.642
## 6 6_month_follow_up                    10.4                0.631

Comparison of environmental factors

### pairwise comparison
# standardize all first
agnes_res_env_std = agnes_res_env %>% mutate(across(3:32, ~ scale(.x, center=F)))
var = colnames(agnes_res_env_std[3:32])

pairwise = function(var) {
  formula = as.formula(paste0("`", var, "` ~ clust"))
  df = agnes_res_env_std[c("clust", var)]
  fit = aov(formula, data = df)
  
  emms = emmeans(fit, "clust")
  pairwise_contrasts = summary(
    contrast(emms, method = "pairwise", adjust = "none"),
    infer = c(TRUE, TRUE)  # ensure CIs and p-values
  ) %>% as.data.frame()
  
  # Rename CI columns if present
  names(pairwise_contrasts)[names(pairwise_contrasts) %in% c("lower.CL", "asymp.LCL")] <- "CI_lower"
  names(pairwise_contrasts)[names(pairwise_contrasts) %in% c("upper.CL", "asymp.UCL")] <- "CI_upper"
  
  # Ensure CI columns exist before selecting
  if (!all(c("CI_lower", "CI_upper") %in% names(pairwise_contrasts))) {
    stop("Confidence intervals not present in summary output.")
  }

  comp = pairwise_contrasts %>%
    rename(
      comparison = contrast,
      mean_diff = estimate
    ) %>%
    mutate(var = var) %>%
    select(var, comparison, mean_diff, SE, CI_lower, CI_upper, p.value)
  
  d_df = eff_size(emms, sigma = sigma(fit), edf = df.residual(fit)) %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    rename(comparison = contrast, cohen_d = effect.size) %>%
    select(comparison, cohen_d)
  
  comp = left_join(comp, d_df, by = "comparison")
  return(comp)
}

pairwise_results = map_dfr(var, pairwise)

pairwise_results %>%
  mutate(pfdr = p.adjust(p.value, method = "fdr")) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))) %>%
  kable("html") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
var comparison mean_diff SE CI_lower CI_upper p.value cohen_d pfdr
